title: Author PROP-042-analyst-compression-mode: the operator-commissioned content-compression analyst mode that operationalizes operator policy D-C (WIN surfaces NEVER content-dense; long debunks compress + link-out rather than expand inline). This is the formal home for the work-shape demonstrated by EXP-401 (WIN-012 1888w → 528w, 3.58×, integrated 66c9a74).
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-042). Operator reviews proposal before implementation amendment is authored. Tinker investigates design surface, proposes canonical mode, does NOT self-apply.
context: The amendment-002 narrowed Category H scope to mechanical link-preview only (anchor-text-bound, no whole-paragraph content compression). The compression-experiment finding (DIRECTIVE-005 addendum) established that real content compression is editorial-judgment work, qualitatively different from the rewriter's mechanical-restructure categories A-H.

EXP-401 demonstrated the work shape end-to-end: HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001 → cowork-claude drafted compressed_html with preservation audit → EXP-401 written with mode='compression' → operator reviewed side-by-side → integrated to wins.json at commit 66c9a74 → follow-up ISS-2027 filed for cut content that was structurally separate.

The Q-OP-A002-4 answer (DIRECTIVE-20260516-007) committed to filing this as a separate PROP rather than bundling into amendment-002 or DIRECTIVE-005 follow-up. Compression mode is enough of a design surface (HNOTE-driven analyst trigger, EXP authorship pattern with mode='compression', decider integration via HOLD-for-operator-review flag, content-preservation discipline that allows paraphrase but not number/citation drift) to warrant its own PROP.

Operator stated preference for 'real rewriting' that produces meaningfully better prose, demonstrated by the EXP-401 result ('much better' per operator review). Phase 2 sloppytoppy-rewrite stays mechanical; compression handled here.

The Phase 2 rewriter is already running (registered 2026-05-16, first run produced 1 RW + 4 PUNTs; 3 of the 4 PUNTs were length-dominated WINs that the rewriter correctly punted — they're exactly the surfaces compression mode addresses).
operator_intent: Tinker authors PROP-042 as a complete design proposal: how compression-mode is triggered (operator HNOTE — pattern matches Mode 5 frozen-pred discipline), the EXP shape (mode='compression', preservation audit fields, cuts_summary structure), decider integration path (HOLD-for-operator-review, not auto-curmudgeon-route), and the relationship to the existing analyst dispatcher (Mode 6 below Mode 5? Or a 'commissioned' priority tier that interacts with DIRECTIVE-006's dispatcher fix?).

tasks_for_tinker.description: Read EXP-401-WIN-012-compression-experiment.json + the HNOTE that commissioned it (HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001) + the integration commit (66c9a74) + the follow-up ISS-2027. This is the worked example. Identify what's mechanical (HNOTE format, EXP schema, integration path) and what's judgment (cuts decisions, link-out targets, follow-up-ISS proposals).
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Short audit summarizing the EXP-401 work shape as it would be spec'd into a recurring analyst mode.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Define the HNOTE structure that invokes compression-mode. Key design questions:
questions: C-Q1: Does the HNOTE need a dedicated `mode: 'compression'` field (parallel to Mode 5's frozen-pred detection), or is it sufficient to detect compression intent from body content?
C-Q2: What's the minimum required HNOTE schema? Word target, preservation list (numbers, citations, verdict, claim), allowed cuts, allowed link-out destinations, hold-for-operator-review flag?
C-Q3: Does compression-mode handle one surface per HNOTE, or can a single HNOTE commission multiple surfaces in one batch?
C-Q4: Cross-agent: should curmudgeon-side or decider-side HNOTEs ever commission compression, or is this analyst-exclusive?
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: HNOTE schema spec + example HNOTE matching EXP-401's actual structure.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Define the EXP-NNN.json shape for compression deliveries. EXP-401 set a working pattern; spec it formally. Key fields: mode='compression', current_word_count, proposed_word_count, target_word_count, compression_ratio, proposed_html, cuts_summary (array with cut/words/destination per item), content_preservation_audit (numbers preserved/dropped, citations preserved/dropped, verdict_unchanged, claim_unchanged, cross_win_refs_preserved, structural_section_ref_preserved, argument_structure_summary), follow_up_issue_proposed (optional, for structurally-separate cuts that deserve own surface), hold_for_operator_review=true.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Full EXP schema + audit-field definitions + integration with existing DATA-SCHEMAS.md.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Define how decider intakes a compression EXP. Key design questions:
questions: D-Q1: HOLD-for-operator-review is the operator's stated preference. Should decider write the EXP to a HOLD queue and not route to curmudgeon until operator approves, or should curmudgeon get a parallel preview that the operator can override?

D-Q2: On operator-approve, decider integrates via the standard wins.json/sections.json edit + test.js gate + commit pattern (same as EXP-401 commit 66c9a74). Spec the additive-edit fields needed (status='integrated', integrated_at, integrated_by).
D-Q3: Compression EXPs can include follow_up_issue_proposed — should decider auto-file the suggested ISS, or surface it to operator for explicit approval?

D-Q4: Anchor-format gotcha (EXP-401 had #part6.html#... vs #... — test.js rejected the former). Does decider need to run an anchor-format normalization before integration, or rely on test.js to catch and operator to fix?
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Decider intake spec including new step or extension of existing Step 1m.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Where does compression-mode sit in the analyst dispatcher? Options:

options.option_A_new_mode_6: New 'Mode 6 — Compression Commissions' positioned just below Mode 5 (frozen-pred). Both are operator-explicit-only, never on cadence. Mode 6 has its own trigger (compression-HNOTE detection).
options.option_B_generalize_mode_5: Generalize Mode 5 from 'frozen prediction drafts only' to 'any operator-explicit commission (frozen-pred, compression, custom-WIN-rewrite, ...)'. Move Mode 5 just below Mode 0. Mode 5's body-detection regex expands to recognize compression intent.
options.option_C_use_DIRECTIVE_006_priority_tier: Wait for DIRECTIVE-006 (analyst HNOTE priority bump) to land. Compression HNOTEs flagged 'commission' priority ride above Mode 1. No new Mode needed; reuses the dispatcher fix.

design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Which option? Tinker recommends one with rationale. Operator preference unstated.

Q2: If Option A, what's the relative priority of Mode 5 vs Mode 6 if both have pending commissions? FIFO? Mode 5 first because it pre-existed? Mode 6 first because compression is the operator's stated near-term focus?
Q3: If Option B, what's the migration plan? Existing 'frozen-pred' HNOTEs need backward-compat detection.
Q4: If Option C, this PROP becomes 'compression-mode is just an HNOTE schema + EXP shape; the dispatcher fix handles priority'. Simpler PROP, but creates dependency on DIRECTIVE-006 landing first.

Q5: Cross-coordinate with PROP-NNN-analyst-operator-commission-priority (DIRECTIVE-006's output) — if Option C is chosen, propose the two PROPs land together to avoid the compression-mode launch waiting on dispatcher PR review.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Steelman the case for NOT building a formal compression-mode. Counters to address:
counter_args_to_address: 'EXP-401 worked because cowork-claude did it directly in-session. Just keep doing that — formalize nothing, accept that operator commissions are operator-direct.' Rebuttal expected: scales poorly. The first run already produced 4 PUNTs of which 3 are length-dominated; if every compression needs a cowork-claude session, we're bottlenecked on operator availability.

'Compression risks scientific-precision drift more than mechanical rewriting does. EXP-401 was reviewed by operator before integration, but at scale that's a lot of operator review. The fix is to reduce the need for compression (better WIN-authoring discipline upfront).' Rebuttal expected: better-upfront-authoring is the right long-term play, but the existing 30+ over-target WIN surfaces exist now and need handling. Discipline applies forward; compression cleans up the backlog.

'Compression-mode as analyst commission overlaps with what the operator can do directly with cowork-claude (as demonstrated by EXP-401). Building infrastructure for a workflow operator can do ad-hoc creates maintenance overhead for negligible automation benefit.' Rebuttal expected: depends on the steady-state compression volume. If 1-2 compressions/month, operator-direct is fine. If 5+/week, automation pays off. Run telemetry for 30 days post-launch to decide.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Author PROP-042 as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, requests amendments if needed, then operator + cowork-claude apply the analyst.md edits + create new infrastructure (compression-mode prompt section, EXP schema docs, decider intake extension).

in_scope_for_tinker: Read EXP-401 + the HNOTE that commissioned it + integration commit 66c9a74 + ISS-2027
Read analyst.md Mode 5 for the closest precedent pattern (operator-explicit-only mode with body-detection trigger)
Read DATA-SCHEMAS.md for existing EXP schemas to extend
Read DIRECTIVE-006 (parallel-track dispatcher fix) for the coupling question in task_5
Author one PROP-042 with the full design
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify analyst.md or any prompt directly. Propose-only.
Do NOT propose changes to the rewriter (sloppytoppy-rewrite.md) — compression-mode is analyst infrastructure, not rewriter infrastructure.
Do NOT propose changes to DIRECTIVE-006's work product (the dispatcher fix). Task_5 may cross-reference but compression-mode PROP-042 is self-contained on the placement question.
Do NOT batch with DIRECTIVE-006 or DIRECTIVE-007. PROP-042 is its own design.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: EXP-401 is the worked example — design against that shape, don't invent novel infrastructure
HOLD-for-operator-review is non-negotiable at launch (matches EXP-401's pattern; auto-route to curmudgeon is a future relaxation after track record)

Discipline rule: compression-mode is for OPERATOR-COMMISSIONED work only, not analyst-self-triggered. Operator commissions reflect intentional editorial decisions; analyst-self-triggered would risk runaway compression.
